title: Setup Frame
description: The guided-setup FRAME emitted by engine/setup/flow.computeFrame and surfaced by `engine setup --json`. This is the PUBLISHED CONTRACT a host-runtime adapter (e.g. a Discord component renderer) consumes to draw the changing setup menu: where the operator is on the C0–C4 ladder, what is still needed (the verifier's failing checks + remediation), and the actions available now. The engine owns no Discord connection or token — it emits this frame; a surface renders it. Spec ref: release-spec.md §2.1 resumable setup; §2.8 quick-start; model §12 named-step remediation.

schema_version.description: The frame contract version. A surface pins to this to detect a frame shape it does not understand.

generated_for.description: The step this frame addresses: a checkpoint id, or 'done' for the terminal operational frame.

done.description: True when the whole C0–C4 ladder passes and the project is operational.

headline.description: A one-sentence 'where you are' summary for terse surfaces.

body.description: Plain-language explanation lines for this step (renderer joins with newlines).

todo.description: Outstanding work: the owning checkpoint verifier's failing checks, each with its named remediation.

satisfied.description: The verifier's passing checks for this step (momentum signal).

skipped.description: The verifier's non-failing skip checks (deferrable-until-LIVE items).

actions.description: The actions available now. A surface renders these as numbered commands (CLI) or buttons (Discord).

next_command.description: The single most useful command string for this frame, or null.

just_passed.description: Checkpoints confirmed-passing during this compute walk (the advancement signal).

doc.description: A repo-relative doc path with more detail, or null.

note.description: An optional advisory (e.g. CONTENT_HOME-unset progress-not-saved notice), or null.

spends.description: True when the action costs engine-metered money — the surface MUST surface the estimate-and-confirm gate (DD-18). The engine never auto-spends behind an action.
